stories.headline: Iran war day 103: US strikes after helicopter shot down, Tehran hits back
stories.summary: Analysts warn of further escalation after tit-for-tat attacks between Tehran and Washington.

stories.headline: Explosion in Bahrain as Iran attacks US bases
stories.summary: CCTV footage shows an explosion in Bahrain’s capital Manama after what authorities say was an Iranian missile strike.

stories.headline: Europe Today: US and Iran exchange fresh attacks as EU unveils new Russia sanctions
stories.summary: Iran has launched a missile attack on a US air base in Jordan after President Donald Trump blamed Tehran for the downing of an American Apache helicopter. In Europe, Jorge Liboreiro reports on the EU's new package of sanctions against Russia targeting oil, the Russian "shadow fleet", and banks.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran in response to downing of military helicopter
stories.summary: President Donald Trump earlier accused Iran of shooting down the US helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed to respond.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes after downing of American helicopter
stories.summary: Tehran says it targeted US military facilities in Jordan and Bahrain following latest barrage

stories.headline: Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate primary, vows to oust Collins
stories.summary: Platner wins Maine Senate primary despite controversies, setting up a key race against Republican Susan Collins.

stories.headline: Iran attacks Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan in retaliation for US strikes
stories.summary: Strikes come after US attacked Iranian ports and islands in the Strait of Hormuz over the downing of a helicopter.

stories.headline: Tehran fires missiles at Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain after renewed US strikes on Iran
stories.summary: Jordan intercepted five missiles aimed at an area hosting a major airbase near Azraq while Kuwait and Bahrain said their air defences were engaging incoming fire. The attacks followed US strikes on Iranian air defence and radar sites near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Ukrainian drones strike Sevastopol museum and key Russian oil refineries
stories.summary: The multiday campaign has killed a rail worker, closed the bombed Chongar road bridge and caused a fuel crisis

stories.headline: Somalia criticises US decision to bar World Cup referee

stories.headline: Cars and homes burn as unrest erupts in Belfast after knife attack
stories.summary: Multiple fires burned across parts of Belfast as anti-immigration protests erupted following a stabbing attack. Footage showed burning vehicles, damaged homes and emergency crews responding overnight.

stories.headline: Wall Street’s IPO boom threatens era of shrinking US stock supply
stories.summary: Market debuts of SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI plus slowing buybacks could remove vital support for equities, investors warn

stories.headline: Korea Stock Hedging Surges to Level That Warned of Past Selloffs
stories.summary: The number of bearish options tied to South Korea’s Kospi 200 Index has climbed so sharply relative to bullish wagers that it is nearing a level that previously foreshadowed market declines.

stories.summary: Anna Edwards, Tom Mackenzie and Mark Cudmore break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Platner Wins Maine Senate Primary After a Turbulent Stretch of His Bid

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham Wins Republican Senate Primary in South Carolina
stories.summary: Mr. Graham, a four-term incumbent, is the favorite to win the general election in a solidly Republican state.

stories.headline: 5 Things to Know About Graham Platner, Democratic Senate Candidate in Maine
stories.summary: Mr. Platner, an oyster farmer running for a Senate seat on a progressive platform, has drawn a large following among Democrats in Maine. His campaign has also surfaced damaging reports about his personal history.

stories.headline: 5 Things to Know About Susan Collins, Republican Senate Candidate in Maine
stories.summary: Ms. Collins, the longest-serving Republican woman in Senate history, is also her party’s most vulnerable incumbent this year.

stories.summary: Argentina ease past Iceland in their final friendly before the World Cup, winning 3-0 in Auburn, Alabama.

stories.headline: Afghanistan says Pakistan air raids killed 13 people, including children
stories.summary: No immediate comment from Pakistan as Taliban says 11 children are among the dead in strikes on border provinces.

stories.headline: Malaysia's Anwar warns against global powers weaponizing trade

stories.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung visits the Belgian capital on Wednesday for a highly anticipated EU-South Korea Summit. Onlookers expect a buffet of East Asian strength on display, ranging from K-pop toppers to military might, and closer Brussels-Seoul relations.

stories.headline: Heatwaves are killing tens of thousands in India. Officials are barely counting them
stories.summary: Experts warn that official heatwave death figures are grossly underestimated.

stories.headline: S. Africa’s Eskom Spinoff Plan Raises Credit Concerns
stories.summary: South Africa’s plan to split power utility Eskom Holdings SOC Ltd. into separate units could weaken the state-owned electricity utility’s risk profile because its transmission unit accounts for a major share of its earnings, according to a bondholder and a ratings agency.

stories.headline: Malaysia Palm Reserves Jump Most in Five Months as Exports Drop
stories.summary: Palm oil stockpiles in Malaysia climbed at the fastest pace in five months as a sharp drop in exports overshadowed weaker production in the world’s second-largest grower.

stories.headline: Banks Pay 7% on Dollar Deposits as India Seeks Fresh Foreign Currency
stories.summary: Indian lenders have sharply raised rates on foreign-currency deposits to lure the nation’s overseas residents, offering an early test of the central bank’s recent measures to bring in capital flows to support the rupee.

stories.headline: Hedge Funds on Asia Alpha
stories.summary: Sam Kellie-Smith, CEO & Chief Investment Officer, APAC, Jain Global and Kenny Lam, CEO, Two Sigma Asia Pacific discuss how both liquidity, and opportunities, have grown in Asia with Bloomberg’s Erik Schatzker at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Edaa on Market Infrastructure and Capital Flows
stories.summary: Hanan Mohammad AlShehri, CEO, Securities Depository Center Company (Edaa), Saudi Tadawul Group discusses opening Saudi Arabia’s capital markets to foreign investors with Bloomberg’s Lulu Chen at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Nippon Steel Gets ¥90 Billion Via First Bond Since US Steel
stories.summary: Nippon Steel Corp. raised ¥90 billion ($560 million) via its first straight-bond offering since completing the acquisition of United States Steel Corp.

stories.headline: In Conversation With Clara Chan
stories.summary: Clara Chan, CEO, Hong Kong Investment Corporation Ltd. discusses strategic early investment in AI and large language models with Bloomberg’s Stephen Engle at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Korea’s Kospi Extends Wild Swings as Chip Stocks Resume Slide
stories.summary: South Korea’s stock market lurched from one extreme to another for a third straight session Wednesday, extending a stretch of swings that has whipsawed investors and repeatedly triggered market safeguards.

stories.headline: Lenovo on Strategy and Growth
stories.summary: Winston Cheng, Senior Vice President & Chief Financial Officer, Lenovo said the AI infrastructure boom is probably “the greatest infrastructure build in the history of mankind,”, he was discussing the company outlook with Bloomberg’s Sangmi Cha at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Apollo on Asia Private Markets
stories.summary: Eiji Ueda, Partner & Head of Asia Pacific, Apollo told Bloomberg’s Erik Schatzker that the region’s aging population will create demand for retirement products. He was speaking at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: PAG on Private Capital in Asia
stories.summary: Chris Gradel, CEO & Co-Founder, PAG discusses private capital, liquidity and Asia investment opportunities with Bloomberg’s David Ingles at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump and Netanyahu wanted to reshape the Middle East - now they risk a permacrisis

stories.headline: Inside Myanmar, rebels are losing ground as military forces men into army

stories.headline: Man shot dead during protest against proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya
stories.summary: Police dispersed demonstrators in Nanyuki, 120 miles from Nairobi, amid rising anger at US plansKenyan police have shot dead a man during a protest against a proposed Ebola quarantine facility for US citizens.Patrick Wahome, who has organised protests in Nanyuki against the centre, told Reuters on Tuesday the man died from a gunshot wound to the head. Reporters from the agency saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound.

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

stories.headline: Ragas and symphonies: Indian maestro Ilaiyaraaja is still reshaping music 50 years on
stories.summary: Fifty years after his debut, Ilaiyaraaja remains one of the most celebrated composers in Indian cinema.
